input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>A static promotional image shows a person wearing a camouflage baseball cap with "NEW YORK UNITED" embroidered in white. The brim shadows the face heavily. Top left text reads "PAPOOSE HOODIE SEASON PT 3," with "JANUARY" top right. Bottom text says "HOSTED BY DJ KAYSLAY." The background is dark and indistinct. No visual changes occur.</think><answer></answer>

[34-38] The individual sits before the red wall and tool chest as a red lower-third graphic appears at the bottom left displaying \"Papoose\" and \"Rapper\" in white text while the person gestures with their right hand."}}
</tool_call>

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>

- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-8] A static promotional image remains fixed on screen for the entire duration, showing no movement or lighting shifts. The central focus is a person wearing a camouflage baseball cap featuring the text "NEW YORK UNITED" embroidered in large white letters, with a small star symbol centered between the lines. The cap's brim casts a deep shadow, effectively obscuring the wearer's eyes and upper face. Several text overlays are permanently visible: "PAPOOSE HOODIE SEASON PT 3" appears in a stacked format in the top left corner, "JANUARY" is displayed in the top right, and "HOSTED BY DJ KAYSLAY" runs along the bottom edge in white capital letters. The background is dark and indistinct, possibly urban, providing high contrast for the white text and cap details. The visual display holds steady on this artwork throughout the clip without any transitions.

[14-16] Following a brief glitch of horizontal lines, a person in a red hoodie and black beanie appears standing indoors, gesturing near a table illuminated by a bright rear light. [16-18] A second individual wearing a light grey hoodie and a black cap featuring "ANT" text sits on the right, holding a phone to their ear while adjusting their hat. To the left, the person in red stands beside a black tool chest covered in stickers and topped with various supplies, with a painting of a skull visible in the background. [18-20] The seated person continues their call, raising their left hand to gesture, while the standing figure remains partially visible near the chest. [20-22] The seated individual turns away, displaying the back of their hood, as the standing figure reaches a black-gloved hand toward a tray of items on the tool chest.

[12-14] The white text "6CHAMBER PRODUCTIONS" scales up significantly over a black-and-white background featuring a director's chair and a cap embroidered with "@6CHAMBER". [22-24] The scene cuts to a wall covered in black graffiti tags including "TEDDY NAPS", followed by a vibrant painting of two figures in sunglasses rendered in greens and oranges. [24-28] The view shifts to a location identified by the text "BLACK INK HARLEM, NYC". This sequence shows orange and red wall panels, an orange silhouette graphic, and a red wall where large block letters spelling "BLACK" are filled with intricate tattoo designs like skulls and koi fish, next to a monochromatic cityscape mural.

[30-32] The view shifts to close-ups of artwork on red walls, featuring a large mural of a bearded man, a small framed piece with red abstract lines, and a painting of a woman with long dark hair near shelves. [32-34] After blurry movement, a person wearing a grey hooded shirt, black cap with white text, and sunglasses points forward while seated near a black tool chest under a bright light, with a painting of a figure in a yellow hat behind them. [34-38] The individual sits before the red wall and tool chest as a red lower-third graphic appears at the bottom left displaying "Papoose" and "Rapper" in white text while the person gestures with their right hand.

recent_thinks: [8-10] The previous artwork is replaced by a black background featuring large, white, bold text reading "6CHAMBER PRODUCTIONS". The letters are arranged in two lines, centered on the screen. This production logo completely obscures the prior image of the individual wearing the camouflage cap and associated text overlays. The font is sans-serif and blocky.

[10-12] The previous image cuts to large white text reading "6CHAMBER PRODUCTIONS" on a black background. The text then shrinks and centers over a grainy, black-and-white background scene depicting film equipment and a director's chair with visible horizontal scanlines.

[28-30] The scene cuts to a wide interior shot of a room with bright red walls adorned with large, colorful paintings. A black leather couch sits centrally with a person reclining, while a red tufted booth to the right holds bags. To the left, a bar area is visible with people, and a tall white lamp stands near the wall.

[38-40] A person in a red hoodie and black beanie leans forward, wearing black gloves and using a tattoo machine on the arm of the seated individual in the grey hoodie. The seated person looks down at the process while the red lower-third graphic identifying 'Papoose Rapper' remains visible at the bottom left.

queries.question: What color is the hooded sweatshirt worn by the person holding the tattoo machine? A. Light grey B. Black C. Red D. Brown

answers.text: C

The artist focuses downward on the procedure as the red lower-third graphic vanishes. The tattoo machine continues operating on the skin.</think><answer></answer>

compressed_segments.text: [0-8] A static promotional image remains fixed on screen for the entire duration, showing no movement or lighting shifts. The central focus is a person wearing a camouflage baseball cap featuring the text "NEW YORK UNITED" embroidered in large white letters, with a small star symbol centered between the lines. The cap's brim casts a deep shadow, effectively obscuring the wearer's eyes and upper face. Several text overlays are permanently visible: "PAPOOSE HOODIE SEASON PT 3" appears in a stacked format in the top left corner, "JANUARY" is displayed in the top right, and "HOSTED BY DJ KAYSLAY" runs along the bottom edge in white capital letters. The background is dark and indistinct, possibly urban, providing high contrast for the white text and cap details. The visual display holds steady on this artwork throughout the clip without any transitions. [14-16] Following a brief glitch of horizontal lines, a person in a red hoodie and black beanie appears standing indoors, gesturing near a table illuminated by a bright rear light. [16-18] A second individual wearing a light grey hoodie and a black cap featuring "ANT" text sits on the right, holding a phone to their ear while adjusting their hat. To

[70-76] A person wearing a grey hoodie over a colorful graphic shirt looks downward with a hand near their face, seated next to an individual in a red hoodie, before the scene cuts to a black screen. [76-78] The individual, now seen wearing a black cap and sunglasses, looks down at a tattoo stencil on their left forearm which rests on a surface covered in clear plastic. In the background, a black tool chest with stickers and tattoo ink bottles sits near a red wall with artwork. [78-80] A second person wearing a red hoodie and black beanie leans in from the right, holding a tattoo machine against the stencil on the arm to initiate the tattooing session.

[80-82] A seated individual in a grey hoodie and sunglasses extends an arm with a tattoo stencil. A second person wearing a red hoodie and black beanie leans in from the right, pressing a tattoo machine against the forearm near a black tool chest. [82-86] Close-ups show a forearm with a reddish area and a rectangular stencil containing text. The view cuts to a smooth arm resting on clear plastic wrap with a small, dark design resembling a rectangle with a cross inside. A black-gloved hand touches the skin near the design. [86-88] The gloved hand holds a tattoo machine with a green grip, beginning to ink the top line of the stencil on the forearm.

[88-90] Close-up tattooing transitions to a wider shot revealing an artist in a red hoodie and black beanie working on a client wearing a grey hoodie, cap, and sunglasses, with a large colorful skull painting behind them. [90-96] The scene cuts to the client standing beside a man in a blue and orange jacket with a "KNICKS" logo and an orange cap. The client displays his wrist tattoo while the man in the jacket smiles and looks down. A mirror in the background reflects a third person holding a camera. The client, revealing a graphic tee under the hoodie, looks directly at the camera while the man in the jacket continues inspecting the arm.

A person in a grey hoodie with a shark-tooth design holds a clear plastic cup beside an individual in a blue and orange Knicks jacket and orange cap looking at a black smartphone. The person in the grey hoodie extends their left arm forward. The scene shifts to a tattoo session where an artist in a red hoodie and black beanie, wearing black gloves, wipes the client's arm with a white cloth while holding a tattoo machine. The client wears the grey shark-tooth hoodie. A colorful skull painting is visible on the wall behind the artist near a black tool chest.

recent_thinks: [8-10] The previous artwork is replaced by a black background featuring large, white, bold text reading "6CHAMBER PRODUCTIONS". The letters are arranged in two lines, centered on the screen. This production logo completely obscures the prior image of the individual wearing the camouflage cap and associated text overlays. The font is sans-serif and blocky.

[10-12] The previous image cuts to large white text reading "6CHAMBER PRODUCTIONS" on a black background. The text then shrinks and centers over a grainy, black-and-white background scene depicting film equipment and a director's chair with visible horizontal scanlines.

[68-70] A close-up shows hands sketching with a green pencil on white paper beside a grey sleeve with a silver watch. The view cuts to a person in a grey hoodie and colorful shirt looking down with a hand near their chin, next to a red sleeve.

[106-108] The artist in the red hoodie and black beanie continues wiping the client's arm with a white cloth while holding a tattoo machine. The client, wearing a grey hoodie with a shark-tooth hood design, sits still. A colorful skull painting is visible on the wall behind the artist near a black tool chest with bottles.

[108-110] The camera cuts to an extreme close-up of the tattoo on the arm, showing black-gloved fingers stretching the skin. The design features green and reddish shading, revealing detailed ink work previously unseen in the wider shots. The focus shifts entirely to the artwork on the skin, highlighting the colors and lines being applied.

[110-112] The extreme close-up persists on the forearm, where black-gloved hands manipulate the skin around a tattoo featuring green and reddish shading. A tattoo machine with a green grip is held firmly against the arm. The detailed ink work remains the central focus as the artist proceeds with the application.

[112-114] The extreme close-up continues on the forearm, where black-gloved hands stretch the skin near the green and reddish tattoo. The artist's red hoodie sleeve occupies more of the right foreground, partially obscuring the view as the tattoo machine remains active on the skin.

queries.question: What entities are present and what are they doing?

answers.text: The provided evidence does not support the described scene of a tattoo session. Instead, the video displays a static promotional image, followed by the "6CHAMBER PRODUCTIONS" logo over film equipment. Later segments show graffiti walls with "TEDDY NAPS," colorful paintings of figures with sunglasses, and murals in Harlem featuring tattoo-style art within the word "BLACK." No actual people performing tattoos or holding phones are visible in the available clips.

samples.output:
<think>The artist's red hoodie sleeve moves closer to the camera lens, increasingly obstructing the view of the tattooing process. Black-gloved hands continue working on the forearm featuring green and reddish shading, but the red fabric dominates the right foreground, blurring slightly as it encroaches on the frame and hides the tattoo machine.</think><tool_call>
{"name": "compress", "arguments": {"time_range": [106, 114], "text": "[106-108] An artist wearing a red hoodie and black beanie wipes a client's arm with a white cloth while holding a tattoo machine; the client, dressed in a grey hoodie with a shark-tooth hood design, sits still near a wall featuring a colorful skull painting and a black tool chest with bottles. [108-114] The camera cuts to an extreme close-up of the forearm where black-gloved fingers stretch the skin around a tattoo design featuring green and reddish shading. A tattoo machine with a green grip is held firmly against the arm, highlighting detailed ink work. The artist's red hoodie sleeve occupies the right foreground, partially obscuring the view as the machine remains active on the skin, continuing the application process."}}
</tool_call>

   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

Instead, the video displays a static promotional image, followed by the "6CHAMBER PRODUCTIONS" logo over film equipment. Later segments show graffiti walls with "TEDDY NAPS," colorful paintings of figures with sunglasses, and murals in Harlem featuring tattoo-style art within the word "BLACK." No actual people performing tattoos or holding phones are visible in the available clips.
